name: GD Screen Recorder
description: Native Geometry Dash framebuffer recorder powered by Eclipse FFmpeg API.

toggle-menu-keybind.name: Open Recorder Menu
toggle-menu-keybind.description: Opens the GD Screen Recorder menu, where you can start/stop recording and adjust settings, from anywhere in the game.

fps.name: Frame rate
fps.description: Frames written per second. Recording above the game's rendered frame rate cannot create missing frames.

queue-depth.name: Encoder queue depth
queue-depth.description: Number of reusable frames buffered for the encoder thread. When full, new frames are dropped instead of blocking gameplay.

codec.name: FFmpeg codec
codec.description: Exact video encoder name exposed by the FFmpeg API. libx264 is the broadest MP4-compatible default; hardware H.264 encoders reduce CPU use when available.

container.name: Container extension
container.description: Output filename extension without a dot.

capture-game-audio.name: Record game audio
capture-game-audio.description: Taps Geometry Dash's FMOD master channel group while recording and mixes the captured audio into the finished video. Disable this if you only want a silent video.

capture-microphone.name: Record microphone
capture-microphone.description: Captures the selected FMOD microphone device and mixes it with game audio into the final MP4.

microphone-device-guid.name: Microphone device
microphone-device-guid.description: FMOD recording-device GUID. Use the F8 menu's microphone button to cycle through detected devices.

audio-file.name: Audio file override path
audio-file.description: Optional stereo audio file mixed into the finished video instead of the captured game audio. Leave empty to use the captured game audio (if enabled above).
